data.content:
Plan for foolery-988b (canonical OpenCode model extractor):

APPROACH: Add normalizeOpenCodeModel(rawModel) to src/lib/agent-identity.ts mirroring the existing per-provider normalisers (Claude/Codex/Gemini). It parses on '/' splits with these rules:
- 3+ tokens (<router>/<vendor>/<model-with-version>): emits model = full input string (preserved verbatim), flavor = first token (router), version = trailing numeric extracted from last token via regex.
- 2 tokens (<vendor>/<model>): emits model = full input string, no flavor (no router known), version = trailing numeric from last token.
- 1 token (bare 'kimi'): emits model = bare string, no flavor, version if numeric trails.
- empty/whitespace-only: returns {} (nothing to canonicalise).

VERSION EXTRACTION RULE: After splitting the trailing token on '-', take all suffix segments that match /^\d+(?:\.\d+)*$/ and join with dots. Examples:
- 'kimi-k2.6' -> last segment is 'k2.6'; '2.6' is the numeric tail (k prefix stripped) -> regex match against the trailing /(\d+(?:\.\d+)*)\$/ gives '2.6'.
- 'claude-sonnet-4-5' -> trailing numeric segments are '4', '5'; joined with dots -> '4.5'.
- 'glm-5.1' -> trailing '5.1' -> '5.1'.
- 'devstral-2512' -> trailing '2512' -> '2512'.
- 'kimi' alone -> no numeric -> no version.

Per AC, NEVER read process.env, OpenCode binary version, runtime hints. Input is rawModel only.

WIRING: Replace the 7-line passthrough in normalizeAgentIdentity (agent-identity.ts:243-250) with a call to normalizeOpenCodeModel; result merged with explicit version override (caller-supplied agent.version still wins per the existing precedence pattern).

FILES TOUCHED:
- src/lib/agent-identity.ts: add normalizeOpenCodeModel function (~30 lines), wire into normalizeAgentIdentity.
- src/lib/__tests__/agent-identity-opencode.test.ts (NEW): describe('normalizeOpenCodeModel', ...) cases for all canonical shapes plus edges (empty, whitespace, malformed). Also a describe('normalizeAgentIdentity OpenCode branch', ...) that asserts end-to-end the version is now derived (not leaked from agent.version).
- src/lib/__tests__/agent-identity.test.ts: fix the 'normalizes OpenCode agent identity' test (line 70-85) — it currently expects passthrough behaviour with version='4'; after the change the parser will derive '4' from claude-sonnet-4 as expected by the contract.

RISK: Existing OpenCode tests in agent-identity-display-parts.test.ts use parseAgentDisplayParts (display path) — they should not change. Tests in agent-identity.test.ts that expect raw passthrough WILL change behaviour. The 'normalizes OpenCode agent identity' test currently asserts agentVersion='4' for input model='copilot/anthropic/claude-sonnet-4', version='4'; after fix the extractor derives version='4' from the path so that test still passes.

NEW BEHAVIOUR FOR THE BUG IN AC: input { command: 'opencode', model: 'openrouter/moonshotai/kimi-k2.6', version: '4.7' (leaked binary version) } -> output { provider: 'OpenCode', model: 'openrouter/moonshotai/kimi-k2.6', flavor: 'openrouter', version: '2.6' }. The leaked '4.7' is replaced.

PRECEDENCE: For OpenCode branch I'll mirror the existing Claude/Codex/Gemini shape — version: cleanValue(agent.version) ?? normalized.version. Wait, the AC says NEVER pull version from runtime hints. But agent.version on the input AgentIdentityLike is canonical already (it comes from registered config). The leaked-4.7 problem is that something upstream sets agent.version to the OpenCode binary version. The fix is two-layered: this knot fixes the parser; sibling knots fix the upstream callers. For now, the SAFE choice is to PREFER the parsed version over agent.version for OpenCode (i.e. version: normalized.version ?? cleanValue(agent.version)) so the binary-version leak is masked even before upstream fixes. Document this asymmetry in a comment near the OpenCode branch.

TEST STRATEGY:
1. Pure unit tests for normalizeOpenCodeModel covering every AC shape + edges.
2. Integration test for normalizeAgentIdentity OpenCode branch that asserts a leaked version (4.7) is overridden by the parsed path version (2.6) — this is the literal regression case from the screenshot.
3. Check existing agent-identity.test.ts case still passes; tweak its expectations only if behaviour materially changes (it should not for the claude-sonnet-4 case).

QUALITY GATES:
- bun run lint
- bunx tsc --noEmit
- bun run test
- bun run build

Risk assessment: LOW. Pure addition + wired-in replacement. No callers outside agent-identity.ts touch normalizeOpenCodeModel directly. The only behaviour change is the OpenCode branch of normalizeAgentIdentity, which fixes a known bug.
